qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Recursive fragment-arguments rewrite causes pre-validation denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the `useFragmentArguments` parse-rewrite loop in `packages/plugins/fragment-arguments/src/*`, not the `graphql-modules` singleton-provider context race fixed by replacing the old `useGraphQLModules` context/controller lifecycle.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Aliased PRIVATE fields bypass the no-session response-cache guard
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an alias-sensitive cache-guard flaw in `packages/plugins/response-cache/src/plugin.ts`; it does not involve `@ExecutionContext()`, concurrent request context bleed, or the `useGraphQLModules` execution/subscription binding bug from the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-h3hw-29fv-2x75 is a race in `@envelop/graphql-modules` where `useGraphQLModules(application)` misbinds singleton-provider `@ExecutionContext()` across concurrent requests; the final qualifying findings in this run instead cover a fragment-arguments parse-loop DoS and a response-cache alias bypass.
evidence: The advisory patch `ab49fa259a51d976c437e084114e070b99720ba9` changes `packages/plugins/graphql-modules/src/index.ts`, removing the old `onContextBuilding`/controller cleanup flow and switching `useGraphQLModules` to `app.createExecution()` and `app.createSubscription()` to bind context to async execution.

`fragment_arguments_recursive_dos` cites `packages/plugins/fragment-arguments/src/index.ts`, `packages/plugins/fragment-arguments/src/utils.ts`, and `packages/core/src/orchestrator.ts`; its root cause is recursive AST rewriting before validation, not request-context propagation.

`response_cache_private_alias_bypass` cites `packages/plugins/response-cache/src/plugin.ts`; its root cause is alias-sensitive `PRIVATE` cache classification on the no-session path, not mixed `@ExecutionContext()` state across parallel requests.
